q.en: Where did Sonia Sotomayor grow up?
q.zh: 索尼娅·索托马约尔是在哪里长大的？

options.en: Puerto Rico according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 波多黎各（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The Bronx neighborhood of New York City

explain.en: The book says Sonia 'grew up in the Bronx neighborhood of New York City.'
explain.zh: 书中说索尼娅'在纽约市布朗克斯社区长大'。

q.en: What disease did Sonia learn she had at age eight?
q.zh: 索尼娅八岁时得知自己患了什么病？

options.en: A broken leg according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 腿骨折（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Doctors told her at eight she had 'Type 1 diabetes,' a serious disease.
explain.zh: 医生在她八岁时告知她得了'1型糖尿病'，一种严重的病。

q.en: Why did Sonia change her dream from detective to lawyer?
q.zh: 索尼娅为什么把梦想从侦探改成律师？

options.en: Doctors said her diabetes meant she should not be a detective
options.zh: 医生说她的糖尿病意味着她不宜当侦探

options.en: She failed detective school according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她没考上侦探学校（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The text says doctors told her she should not be a detective, so 'She changed her dream' to becoming a lawyer.
explain.zh: 文中说医生告诉她不宜当侦探，于是'她改变了梦想'去当律师。

q.en: Whom did President Obama choose Sonia to replace on the Supreme Court?
q.zh: 奥巴马总统选索尼娅接替最高法院里的哪位大法官？

options.en: David H. Souter, who was retiring
Sandra Day O'Connor according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 桑德拉·戴·奥康纳（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Justice David H. Souter was retiring, and Obama chose Sonia 'to replace Justice Souter.'
explain.zh: 大法官大卫·H·索特即将退休，奥巴马选索尼娅'接替索特大法官'。

q.en: Why is Sonia's life story important?
q.zh: 为什么索尼娅的人生故事很重要？

options.en: It shows how hard work and perseverance let her achieve her dreams
options.zh: 它展现了努力与坚持如何让她实现梦想

options.en: She discovered a new animal according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她发现了一种新动物（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The conclusion notes she 'made many dreams in her life come true,' inspiring readers with her perseverance.
explain.zh: 结语提到她'让人生中许多梦想成真'，以坚持精神激励读者。

q.en: Who was the first female Supreme Court justice?
q.zh: 第一位女性最高法院大法官是谁？

options.en: Ruth Bader Ginsburg according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 鲁思·巴德·金斯伯格（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states Sandra Day O'Connor 'joined the court as its first female justice in 1981.'
explain.zh: 书中说桑德拉·戴·奥康纳'于1981年作为首位女性大法官加入法院'。

q.en: What made Sonia two 'firsts' when chosen?
q.zh: 索尼娅获选时创下了哪两个'第一'？

options.en: First Latina and first person with diabetes
options.zh: 首位拉丁裔女性，也是首位糖尿病患者

options.en: Youngest justice ever according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 史上最年轻的大法官（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: She would be 'the first Latina and the first person with diabetes to be chosen as a justice.'
explain.zh: 她将是'首位拉丁裔、也是首位糖尿病患者出身的大法官入选者'。

q.en: When did the Senate vote to confirm Sonia?
q.zh: 参议院是在何时投票确认索尼娅的？

explain.en: The text says 'when the Senate voted on August 6, 2009, the majority voted to confirm Judge Sotomayor.'
explain.zh: 文中说'2009年8月6日参议院投票时，多数票确认了索托马约尔法官'。

q.en: Who swore Sonia in as a justice?
q.zh: 是谁主持宣誓，让索尼娅正式成为大法官的？

options.en: Vice President Biden according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 拜登副总统（按书中另一种说法）
首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨

explain.en: Two days after the vote, 'Sonia Sotomayor was sworn in by Chief Justice John Roberts.'
explain.zh: 投票两天后，'索尼娅·索托马约尔由首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨主持宣誓就职'。

q.en: What is the main message of this biography?
q.zh: 这本传记的主旨是什么？

options.en: With effort and support, big dreams can come true
options.zh: 只要努力并有人支持，远大的梦想也能成真

options.en: The Court is only for men
Only presidents become famous according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只有总统才会出名（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book highlights her scholarships, hard work, and historic 'firsts' to show dreams can be reached.
explain.zh: 书中强调她的奖学金、努力和历史性的'第一'，说明梦想可以实现。

prompt.en: Name one challenge Sonia faced and one way she overcame it. What can you learn from her story?
prompt.zh: 说出索尼娅遇到的一个困难，以及她克服它的一种方式。你能从她的故事中学到什么？

en: State a challenge from the book.
Tell how she succeeded and your lesson.
zh: 说明她如何成功，并讲讲你的体会。
